organizations.role: Public supporter / donor (self-stated pro-Israel political interest)
organizations.notes: Loewy Law Firm leadership bio and Texas Hillel alumni spotlight both state AIPAC is among organizations the Loewys actively support. Adam has "keen interest in pro-Israel politics and is involved with AIPAC." Disambiguation: Velasquez batch Loewy, Adam / law loewy / Austin 78731 = Adam Jacob Loewy, Loewy Law Firm P.C.

organizations.role: Former Executive Council Member, Austin Chapter
organizations.notes: Randi Shade's public LinkedIn profile lists her as "former Executive Council Member of Austin Chapter of Anti-Defamation League" (repeatedly indexed in Google search snippets). She and fellow council members Sheryl Cole and Laura Morrison formally engaged ADL Austin in 2010 to develop the city-led hate crimes task force. Disambiguation: only one Randi Shade in Austin 78703 politically active; former City Council Member 2008-2011 and entrepreneur.

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (2009-2012); helped create Austin Hate Crimes Task Force; expanded No Place for Hate program
organizations.notes: Wikipedia biography of Steve Adler (58th Austin mayor 2015-2023) explicitly documents he "served as the board chair of the Anti-Defamation League Austin Region" from 2009 to 2012 and notes ADL board-chair work alongside Texas Tribune/Ballet Austin civic roles. Disambiguation: Fuentes donor listed as "mayor" at Austin 78701 matches Steve Adler, former Austin mayor.
Helped establish the Austin ADL chapter. Per PolitiFact 2014: led board 2009-2012. Per Forward: "He made ADL the go-to civil rights organization in Austin" — Kirk Rudy quote.

Sandy (Sanford) Dochen, longtime Austin civic leader; UT Austin grad, 21-year IBM career as corporate social responsibility/citizenship director (retired 2019), now affiliated with Amplify Credit Union. 78731 (Northwest Hills) matches. Sits on numerous boards. Gave to both Brigid Shea and Ann Howard.

organizations.role: Board Member; National Jewish Democratic Council secretary
organizations.notes: CEO Go! Retail/Calendar Club; documented ADL Austin involvement; also formerly secretary Elie Wiesel Foundation for Humanity

Texas Book Festival board bio explicitly identifies Marc Winkelman as "secretary of the National Jewish Democratic Council". NJDC is a Democratic-aligned Jewish pro-Israel political organization. organizations.role: team member / advocate (oil & gas regulatory accountability)
organizations.notes: Ira John Yates of Paonia, CO 81428 (ZIP matches) is a descendant of rancher/oil pioneer Ira Griffith Yates Jr. and an active mineral-interest owner in the historic Yates Oil Field; he is affiliated with Commission Shift, an oil-and-gas regulatory-accountability advocacy group (orphaned wells, riverbed lease cleanup). Distinctive name + Paonia location + oil heritage corroborate; gave $3,000 to Shea and Howard 2022-2024.
